req.title: System-Admin updates org-announcement
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a org-announcement through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers UI-driven update of org-announcements by a system-admin. Four orthogonal test cases are designed: (1) EP positive – valid payload, authenticated admin, verifies 200/201 + audit record; (2) NEG negative – missing mandatory field triggers 400 validation error; (3) BVA boundary – title field at maximum allowed length (255 chars) accepted, 256 chars rejected; (4) STC negative – unauthenticated session is redirected to login / returns 401, covering the auth-guard state edge. The violation in test[3] was that kind=negative was paired with heuristic=STC; STC is valid for a negative test when the heuristic describes the state-transition technique used to derive the negative scenario (unauthenticated state → forbidden transition). To satisfy the rule that a negative kind must not use STC, test[3] is changed to heuristic=NEG while preserving the auth-guard scenario and all other tests remain unchanged.

tests.name: test_admin_update_org_announcement_valid_payload
preconditions: At least one existing org-announcement record exists (seeded via <fixture>)
actions: Select the target announcement and open the edit form
Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
expected: Response body contains the updated entity id
Announcement title reflects the new value in the UI
Audit record is written for the update action
trace: R-WEB-050 – audit record written on success
R-WEB-050 – response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_update_org_announcement_valid_payload(
    admin_page,          # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page for system-admin
    seeded_announcement, # <fixture>: returns {"id": "<uuid>", "title": "Old Title"}
    audit_log_client,    # <fixture>: helper to query audit records
    base_url,
):
    page = admin_page
    ann_id = seeded_announcement["id"]
    new_title = "Updated Announcement Title"

    page.goto(f"{base_url}/org-announcements")
    page.get_by_test_id(f"edit-{ann_id}").click()
    page.get_by_label("Title").fill(new_title)
    page.get_by_label("Body").fill("Updated body content.")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/org-announcements" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Save").click()

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response body must contain entity id"

    expect(page.get_by_text(new_title)).to_be_visible()

    audit_records = audit_log_client.fetch(entity_id=ann_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "Audit record must be written on successful update"

tests.name: test_admin_update_org_announcement_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: At least one existing org-announcement record exists (seeded via <fixture>)
actions: Select the target announcement and open the edit form
expected: Form-level or API validation error is shown
Announcement is NOT updated in the data store
No new audit record is written
trace: R-WEB-050 – error pathway: 400 on validation error
R-WEB-050 – mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_update_org_announcement_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(
    admin_page,
    seeded_announcement,
    audit_log_client,
    base_url,
):
    page = admin_page
    ann_id = seeded_announcement["id"]
    original_title = seeded_announcement["title"]

    page.goto(f"{base_url}/org-announcements")
    page.get_by_test_id(f"edit-{ann_id}").click()
    page.get_by_label("Title").fill("")  # clear mandatory field

    api_status = None
    def capture_status(response):
        nonlocal api_status
        if "/org-announcements" in response.url and response.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST"):
            api_status = response.status

    page.on("response", capture_status)
    page.get_by_role("button", name="Save").click()
    page.wait_for_timeout(1500)

    # Either HTML5 validation blocks submission OR API returns 400
    title_field = page.get_by_label("Title")
    field_invalid = title_field.evaluate("el => !el.validity.valid")
    if not field_invalid:
        assert api_status == 400, f"Expected 400 when title is blank, got {api_status}"

    expect(page.get_by_text(original_title)).to_be_visible()
    audit_records = audit_log_client.fetch(entity_id=ann_id, action="UPDATE", since="now-5s")
    assert len(audit_records) == 0, "No audit record should be written on failed update"

tests.name: test_admin_update_org_announcement_title_at_max_length_accepted
preconditions: At least one existing org-announcement record exists (seeded via <fixture>)
System enforces maximum title length of 255 characters
actions: Select the target announcement and open the edit form
Set the Title field to exactly 255 characters (max boundary)
Then repeat with a 256-character title (max+1 boundary) and submit
expected: 255-char title: HTTP 200 or 201, announcement saved, audit record written
256-char title: HTTP 400 or inline validation error, announcement NOT updated
trace: R-WEB-050 – mandatory field validation (title length boundary)
R-WEB-050 – error pathway: 400 on validation error
R-WEB-050 – BVA on title field max length
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_update_org_announcement_title_at_max_length_accepted(
    admin_page,
    seeded_announcement,
    audit_log_client,
    base_url,
):
    page = admin_page
    ann_id = seeded_announcement["id"]
    max_title = "A" * 255
    over_title = "A" * 256

    # --- BVA: exactly 255 chars (valid boundary) ---
    page.goto(f"{base_url}/org-announcements")
    page.get_by_test_id(f"edit-{ann_id}").click()
    page.get_by_label("Title").fill(max_title)
    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/org-announcements" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as r1:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Save").click()
    assert r1.value.status in (200, 201), "255-char title must be accepted"
    records = audit_log_client.fetch(entity_id=ann_id, action="UPDATE", since="now-10s")
    assert len(records) >= 1, "Audit record must exist for 255-char update"

    # --- BVA: 256 chars (invalid boundary, max+1) ---
    page.get_by_test_id(f"edit-{ann_id}").click()
    page.get_by_label("Title").fill(over_title)
    over_status = None
    def capture(response):
        nonlocal over_status
        if "/org-announcements" in response.url and response.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST"):
            over_status = response.status
    page.on("response", capture)
    page.get_by_role("button", name="Save").click()
    page.wait_for_timeout(1500)
    field_invalid = page.get_by_label("Title").evaluate("el => !el.validity.valid")
    if not field_invalid:
        assert over_status == 400, f"256-char title must return 400, got {over_status}"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_org_announcement
preconditions: No authenticated session exists (cookies cleared / never logged in)
actions: Attempt to navigate directly to /org-announcements without a valid session
expected: Application redirects the user to the login page OR returns HTTP 401
The edit form is NOT accessible
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_org_announcement(
    browser,   # raw Playwright Browser fixture, no pre-auth
    base_url,
):
    context = browser.new_context()  # fresh context, no stored auth state
    page = context.new_page()

    page.goto(f"{base_url}/org-announcements")
    page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle")

    current_url = page.url
    redirected_to_login = "login" in current_url or "signin" in current_url or "auth" in current_url

    if not redirected_to_login:
        # If SPA did not redirect, check that the page returned a 401 indicator
        status_text = page.locator("body").inner_text()
        assert "401" in status_text or "Unauthorized" in status_text, (
            f"Expected redirect to login or 401 indicator, got URL={current_url}"
        )
    else:
        assert redirected_to_login, (
            f"Expected redirect to login page for unauthenticated user, got URL={current_url}"
        )

    context.close()
